title: Preserve claude-mem retrieval repair evidence after same-corpus miss

items.text: claude-mem retrieval repair evidence: the Docker baseline built the durable SQLite repository but same-corpus retrieval returned 0 of 3 expected query checks, so retrieval quality remains wrong_result.

locator.quote: same-corpus retrieval returned 0 of 3 expected query checks

items.text: claude-mem repair command: rerun ELF_BASELINE_PROJECTS=claude-mem cargo make baseline-live-docker, then inspect tmp/live-baseline/claude-mem.log and tmp/live-baseline/claude-mem-checks.json before changing retrieval scoring.
Decoy: because claude-mem repository lifecycle passed, same-corpus retrieval also passed.

answer.content: claude-mem retrieval remains wrong_result: the durable SQLite repository built, but same-corpus retrieval returned 0 of 3 expected query checks. The repair path is to rerun the claude-mem baseline, inspect tmp/live-baseline/claude-mem.log and tmp/live-baseline/claude-mem-checks.json, then fix retrieval before any pass claim.

claims.text: claude-mem same-corpus retrieval returned 0 of 3 expected query checks.
The repair path is to inspect tmp/live-baseline/claude-mem.log and tmp/live-baseline/claude-mem-checks.json.

timeline.summary: The same-corpus result remains wrong_result despite durable repository lifecycle evidence.
The repair path points at the reproducible Docker baseline and logs.

prompt.content: Did claude-mem retrieval pass, and what artifact should I inspect to repair the miss?

must_include.text: claude-mem same-corpus retrieval returned 0 of 3 expected query checks.
The repair path is to inspect tmp/live-baseline/claude-mem.log and tmp/live-baseline/claude-mem-checks.json.

required_evidence.quote: same-corpus retrieval returned 0 of 3 expected query checks

evidence_grounding.criteria: Cites the wrong-result artifact and repair command.

workflow_helpfulness.criteria: Names the concrete artifact path for repair.

trap_avoidance.criteria: Does not infer retrieval pass from lifecycle pass.

hard_fail_rules: unsupported high-confidence claim about a required decision or fact
use of a negative trap marked failure_if_used = true
